name: KrillApp.Executor.Calculation
title: Calculation
description: Evaluates a mathematical formula that references the last results of the nodes listed in its meta.inputs. Inputs are referenced in the formula with bracketed NodeIdentity tokens of the form [hostId:nodeId] (e.g., [348f…:ab12…]+1), which are replaced with each input's current meta.snapshot value at execution time. Supports arithmetic operators (+, -, *, /, %, ^), mathematical functions (sin, cos, tan, sqrt, abs, ln), and parenthesized expressions. The computed result is stored in the calculation's own meta.snapshot; downstream observers pull it from there.
shortDescription: Computes a mathematical formula from its input nodes' snapshots and stores the result in its own snapshot.
llmPurpose: Evaluates a mathematical formula referencing its input nodes' snapshot values and stores the computed result in its own meta.snapshot for observers to pull.
llmBehavior: invoked when an observed source completes (SOURCE_INVOKED) or on manual click
resolves [hostId:nodeId] tokens in the formula to the current snapshot values of nodes in meta.inputs
stores the result in its own meta.snapshot; nodes observing this calculation are invoked
llmInputs: formula string with [hostId:nodeId] references to nodes listed in meta.inputs
llmOutputs: computed numeric result stored in own meta.snapshot; observers pull it from there
llmTypicalUseCases: derive a calculated value from multiple sensors
llmPromptHints: compute the average of these sensors
llmCreationHints: Create a Calculation when the user needs a derived value from one or more DataPoints using a mathematical formula. Requires a formula string referencing inputs with [hostId:nodeId] tokens (NodeIdentity string form), the referenced DataPoints listed in meta.inputs, and the invoking schedule or trigger listed in meta.sources with SOURCE_INVOKED. To persist the result, wire a DataPoint to observe this calculation (its own sources and inputs).

llmConnectionHints.role: transform node that computes a formula from its inputs and stores the result in its own snapshot
llmConnectionHints.wiring: Observer-side: list the schedule/trigger in sources with SOURCE_INVOKED; list formula DataPoints in inputs; a DataPoint wanting the result observes this calc via its own sources+inputs.

llmExamples: Calculate the sum of these two sensor readings.
Convert this temperature from Celsius to Fahrenheit with [hostId:nodeId] * 9/5 + 32.
Compute the difference between these two data points.
Create a weighted average of three sensors.
